scope: Open-source Nixtla ecosystem for nixtla_scaffold. TimeGPT is excluded from implementation and recommendations.
fppy_citation: Hyndman, R.J., Athanasopoulos, G., Garza, A., Challu, C., Mergenthaler, M., & Olivares, K.G. (2025). Forecasting: Principles and Practice, the Pythonic Way. OTexts: Melbourne, Australia. Available at: OTexts.com/fpppy. Accessed on 28 April 2026.

library_map.default_role: Core statistical model ladder and rolling-origin evaluation engine.
library_map.canonical_contract: Long DataFrame with unique_id, ds, y.

library_map.default_role: Reference for evaluation, losses, plotting, gap filling, and feature utilities.
library_map.canonical_contract: Long DataFrame with actuals and model columns.
library_map.implemented_now: Concepts reflected in WAPE/MAE/bias metrics, backtest receipts, and gap repair; direct utility wrappers are deferred until a demo needs them.

library_map.default_role: Future reconciliation engine after base forecasts and hierarchy metadata are stable.
library_map.canonical_contract: Historical data, base forecasts, summing matrix, and tags.
library_map.implemented_now: Generic hierarchy aggregation, metadata preservation, not-reconciled warning, and coherence diagnostics.

library_map.default_role: Future path for known future drivers/regressors with lags/date/rolling features.
library_map.canonical_contract: Long DataFrame with unique_id, ds, y plus known future exogenous variables.
library_map.implemented_now: DriverEvent scenario overlays are implemented as auditable post-model overlays; ML training is deferred to avoid leakage until known-future driver contracts are proven.

library_map.default_role: Research-only optional future extension for heavier neural models.
library_map.canonical_contract: Long DataFrame with unique_id, ds, y.
library_map.implemented_now: Not in the core package; retained as optional research because finance explainability and dependency weight are concerns.

modeling_policy_summary: Always validate canonical data before modeling.
Always include benchmark forecasts when eligible.
Use rolling-origin backtests when history allows.
Expose intervals only when there is enough history/model support.
Keep driver/event assumptions as explicit scenario overlays.
Treat hierarchy parent/child forecasts as diagnostic until reconciled.
Keep weighted forecasts auditable via model_weights.csv.
Keep diagnostics.json and failure_diagnostics.json easy for future LLMs to parse.
